name: Olèrdola Park and Canyelles Loop
name_original: Parque de Olerdola – circular por los alrededores de Villafranca del Penedés
description: This moderate 15.5-mile road cycling route around Villafranca del Penedés offers scenic vineyards, historic sites, and great views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Olivella are characterized by diverse terrains, including challenging climbs and scenic Mediterranean views. The region's landscape features Garraf Park, offering varied routes with significant elevation gains. Cyclists can expect mostly well-paved surfaces, suitable for road bikes, though some longer segments may include unpaved sections. This area provides a mix of moderate and demanding routes, catering to different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Olivella The most popular road cycling route is Olèrdola Park and Canyelles Loop, a 15.5 miles (24.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 15 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic vineyards and historic sites. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coll de l'Ordal and Olesa de Bonesvalls Loop, a moderate 22.6 miles (36.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Iron sculptures – Castellet Castle loop from Sitges, a 37.3 miles (60.1 km) trail leading through areas with notable landmarks like Castellet Castle, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Olivella is defined by diverse terrains, challenging climbs, and Mediterranean views within Garraf Park. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Olivella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 290 reviews. More than 17,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Olivella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Olivella?
A: There are over 200 road cycling routes around Olivella, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the typical terrains like for road cycling in Olivella?
A: Road cycling routes around Olivella feature diverse terrains, including challenging climbs and scenic Mediterranean views. You'll find mostly well-paved surfaces, suitable for road bikes, though some longer segments might include unpaved sections, particularly within Garraf Park.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Olivella?
A: The region around Olivella is generally favorable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while summer can be warm but still enjoyable, especially during early mornings or late afternoons. Winter provides cooler, crisp conditions.

Q: How challenging are the road cycling routes in Olivella?
A: The routes in Olivella cater to various fitness levels. You can find moderate routes with up to 1,000 feet of elevation gain, as well as more demanding ones that can exceed 3,000 feet, requiring good to very good fitness. There are 25 easy, 126 moderate, and 56 difficult routes available.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass by notable landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24955315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron sculptures – Castellet Castle loop from Sitges&lt;/a&gt; features &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle and the Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes might lead you past &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3922796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olèrdola Castle and Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Buddhist Monastery, Sakya Tashi Ling&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes in Olivella?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer picturesque Mediterranean views, especially those traversing Garraf Park. You can also find routes that lead to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2724790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morella Summit&lt;/a&gt;, providing expansive panoramas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Olivella?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there are 25 easy routes available that could be suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. It's always recommended to check the route details for distance and elevation before setting out.

Q: What are the options for parking when road cycling in Olivella?
A: Parking options vary depending on your starting point. Many routes begin from towns like Sitges or directly from Olivella, where public parking is generally available. For routes within Garraf Natural Park, look for designated parking areas near trailheads.

Q: Is public transport available to access road cycling routes in Olivella?
A: Public transport options, such as buses, connect Olivella and nearby towns like Sitges. Some services may accommodate bicycles, but it's advisable to check with the local transport providers for their specific policies and schedules regarding bicycle carriage.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in Garraf Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on established roads within Garraf Natural Park. However, if you plan to venture onto unpaved tracks or specific protected areas, it's always best to consult the official Garraf Natural Park website for any updated regulations.

Q: What do other road cyclists appreciate about the routes in Olivella?
A: The road cycling routes around Olivella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 290 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrains, challenging climbs, and the stunning Mediterranean views, especially within Garraf Park.

Q: Are there longer, more challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle – Coll de les Ventoses loop from Sitges&lt;/a&gt; offer a significant distance of 100 km and over 1200 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find routes that feature vineyards or historic sites?
A: Certainly. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996519281/olerdola-park-and-canyelles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olèrdola Park and Canyelles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering scenic vineyards and historic sites along its 15.5-mile path. This route allows you to combine your ride with cultural exploration.
tips: Secondary road that takes us inside the Garraf park.

Asphalt road in good condition that connects the towns of Moja and Sant Pere Molanta (both belonging to the municipality of Olèrdola). Little traffic and excellent views of the vineyards, the castle of Olèrdola, or the Puig de l'Aliga, among other points of interest.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This circular racebike route around Villafranca del Penedès usually takes about 1 hour and 15 minutes to complete, covering a distance of nearly 25 kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. It requires good fitness due to its rolling terrain and some elevation gain. While primarily road cycling, be prepared for varied surfaces, as some segments might be rougher than typical paved roads.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in the renowned Penedès wine region, with extensive vineyards, peach trees, and charming small towns. The route also offers exceptionally good views from higher points and passes historical sites like the Olèrdola castle.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest or viewpoints?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features scenic sections like the Sant Pere Molanta to Sant Pere de Ribes Road and the Rossend Montané Path. You'll also encounter the historical Old Castle and a medieval village in the Olèrdola area, providing architectural points of interest and great views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Penedès region is ideal for cycling due to its diverse landscapes. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for cycling, avoiding the peak summer heat and winter chill. This allows for comfortable exploration of the vineyards and historical sites.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the starting point are not provided, Villafranca del Penedès, being a significant town, will have various parking options. It's advisable to look for public parking facilities within the town to begin your circular route.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Parque de Olèrdola?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits required for cycling on public roads and designated paths within the Parque de Olèrdola. However, if you plan to visit the Olèrdola castle or other specific historical sites, there might be an entrance fee for those attractions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is primarily a road cycling route that passes through rural areas and towns. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially near roads or in populated areas. Check local regulations for specific rules within the Parque de Olèrdola if you plan to stop at specific park facilities.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with trails such as the Penedès 360 Mountain Bike (ruta 5 dies), Camí Ramader de Marina, and the Penedès 360 Slow Bike, offering opportunities to explore further if desired.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the way?
faqs.answer: The Penedès region is known for its strong wine and gastronomy culture. As the route circles Villafranca del Penedès and passes through smaller towns, you'll likely find cafes, restaurants, and bodegas where you can stop for refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by rolling hills, typical of the Penedès wine region. While mostly asphalt, some segments may be unpaved or have varied surfaces, requiring a racebike suitable for such conditions. The area features a mix of quiet, well-maintained secondary roads and asphalt paths.
